tests.transcription: In nineteen thirty-three you and I knew that we must never let our economic system get completely out of joint again

tests.transcription: It was read for the first time in March eighteen sixty-six, but did not become law till eighteen sixty-eight.

exercise.instruction: Listen to the audio and choose the correct answer.
exercise.question: How many years passed between when it was first read and when it became law?

tests.transcription: Its outer part, about twelve feet in width, was protected with towers at intervals of sixty-five feet.

tests.transcription: I am reminded of that evening in March, four years ago, when I made my first radio report to you.

tests.transcription: It helped him in his job here, he was able to come back here with a fresh view of many things.
